title: StackPicks · Three.js + R3F — 3D in your React app

rows.title: Three.js handles the math
rows.desc: Industry-standard WebGL renderer. Every 3D website you've seen uses it.

rows.title: @react-three/drei = helpers
rows.desc: Cameras, controls, loaders, post-FX. The bits that take days to wire by hand.

install.body: @react-three/fiber bridges React ↔ Three. @react-three/drei is the helper library — 100+ pre-built bits.

rows.title: R3F wins for
rows.desc: React apps. State-driven scenes. Hot reload. Component reuse. Performance is identical.

rows.title: Raw Three wins for
rows.desc: Games. Non-React apps. When every frame counts and React's reconciler is overhead.

rows.title: vs Babylon.js
rows.desc: Babylon is heavier but has built-in physics + animation editor. Three is leaner + bigger ecosystem.

take.use_if: You're on React + Next.js + want 3D hero scenes, product viewers, or interactive visualizations. R3F is the path of least pain.
take.skip_if: You're shipping a 2D landing page. 3D loads slowly on mobile. Don't add it because it looks cool — add it because it tells the story.

more.body: GLTF loaders · Spline · Lottie · Rive · PlayCanvas. Curated takes.
more.sub: 9 picks · all open source · updated this week

caption_line: WebGL but it's JSX — React Three Fiber. The whole 3D web (Spline, Vercel, Apple's site) uses it under the hood. Comment THREE for the docs ↓
